context: Tests whether the agent rejects blanket rule disabling and provides narrow inline suppressions with rationale. The key behavior is explaining WHY disabling rule groups is wrong and showing biome-ignore comments with specific rule names.

checklist.name: Blanket approach explained as wrong
checklist.description: SUPPRESSIONS.md explains that disabling rules globally in biome.json hides defects across all files, not just the intended location

checklist.name: Correct biome-ignore syntax shown
checklist.description: SUPPRESSIONS.md shows the biome-ignore comment syntax (e.g., // biome-ignore lint/suspicious/noExplicitAny: <reason>) for at least one case

checklist.name: noExplicitAny suppression references ticket
checklist.description: The suppression for noExplicitAny includes a reason that mentions the follow-up ticket (JIRA-1234 or similar reference)

checklist.name: noConsole suppression includes operational reason
checklist.description: The suppression for noConsole includes a reason explaining the intentional nature (e.g., operator alert, hash collision warning)

checklist.name: Both suppressions are inline (not global)
checklist.description: Both suppressions are shown as line-level comments, not as biome.json rule changes
